title: Beyond the Golden Data: Resolving the Motion-Vision Quality Dilemma via Timestep Selective Training
summary: Recent advances in video generation models have achieved impressive results. However, these models heavily rely on the use of high-quality data that combines both high visual quality and high motion quality. In this paper, we identify a key challenge in video data curation: the Motion-Vision Quality Dilemma. We discovered that visual quality and motion intensity inherently exhibit a negative correlation, making it hard to obtain golden data that excels in both aspects. To address this challenge, we first examine the hierarchical learning dynamics of video diffusion models and conduct gradient-based analysis on quality-degraded samples. We discover that quality-imbalanced data can produce gradients similar to golden data at appropriate timesteps. Based on this, we introduce the novel concept of Timestep selection in Training Process. We propose Timestep-aware Quality Decoupling (TQD), which modifies the data sampling distribution to better match the model's learning process. For certain types of data, the sampling distribution is skewed toward higher timesteps for motion-rich data, while high visual quality data is more likely to be sampled during lower timesteps. Through extensive experiments, we demonstrate that TQD enables training exclusively on separated imbalanced data to achieve performance surpassing conventional training with better data, challenging the necessity of perfect data in video generation. Moreover, our method also boosts model performance when trained on high-quality data, showcasing its effectiveness across different data scenarios.

interest_filter.reason: 该论文直接针对视频生成模型的数据质量挑战，提出了一种新的训练方法来解决运动-视觉质量困境。这与用户的"Video Generation"兴趣高度相关，涉及视频生成模型的核心训练技术。论文研究视频扩散模型的学习动态，并提出时间步选择训练方法，这直接属于视频生成领域的前沿研究。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种解决视频生成中"运动-视觉质量困境"的新方法TQD，通过时间步感知的质量解耦，能够在质量不平衡的数据上训练出超越传统高质量数据训练的模型性能。
AI.motivation: 视频生成模型需要同时具备高视觉质量和高运动质量的数据，但研究发现视觉质量和运动强度存在负相关关系，难以获得两者都优秀的"黄金数据"，这构成了视频数据策展中的核心挑战。
AI.method: 首先分析了视频扩散模型的分层学习动态，对质量退化样本进行基于梯度的分析。发现质量不平衡数据在适当的时间步可以产生与黄金数据相似的梯度。基于此提出了时间步感知质量解耦(TQD)方法，修改数据采样分布以更好地匹配模型学习过程：运动丰富的数据偏向更高时间步采样，而高视觉质量数据在较低时间步更可能被采样。
AI.result: 实验表明，TQD能够在分离的不平衡数据上训练，达到超越传统使用更好数据训练的性能，挑战了视频生成中完美数据的必要性。同时，该方法在高质量数据上训练时也能提升模型性能，展示了在不同数据场景下的有效性。
AI.conclusion: TQD方法通过时间步选择策略有效解决了视频生成中的运动-视觉质量困境，不仅能够在质量不平衡数据上取得优异性能，还能提升高质量数据训练的效果，为视频生成模型的训练提供了新的数据利用策略。

title: PAWS: Perception of Articulation in the Wild at Scale from Egocentric Videos
summary: Articulation perception aims to recover the motion and structure of articulated objects (e.g., drawers and cupboards), and is fundamental to 3D scene understanding in robotics, simulation, and animation. Existing learning-based methods rely heavily on supervised training with high-quality 3D data and manual annotations, limiting scalability and diversity. To address this limitation, we propose PAWS, a method that directly extracts object articulations from hand-object interactions in large-scale in-the-wild egocentric videos. We evaluate our method on the public data sets, including HD-EPIC and Arti4D data sets, achieving significant improvements over baselines. We further demonstrate that the extracted articulations benefit downstream tasks, including fine-tuning 3D articulation prediction models and enabling robot manipulation. See the project website at https://aaltoml.github.io/PAWS/.

interest_filter.reason: 该论文研究从第一人称视频中提取物体关节运动，直接涉及3D重建、几何理解、场景理解等核心兴趣。论文关注关节物体的运动和结构恢复，这与用户的3D生成、3D重建、几何分析等兴趣高度相关。虽然论文主要关注关节感知而非视频生成，但其从视频中提取3D运动信息的方法与用户的视频生成、4D动态重建等兴趣有直接联系。

AI.tldr: PAWS方法从大规模第一人称视角视频中的手-物体交互直接提取物体关节结构，无需高质量3D数据和人工标注
AI.motivation: 现有基于学习的方法严重依赖高质量3D数据和人工标注的监督训练，限制了可扩展性和多样性
AI.method: 直接从大规模野外第一人称视角视频中的手-物体交互提取物体关节结构
AI.result: 在HD-EPIC和Arti4D数据集上相比基线方法取得显著改进，提取的关节结构有益于下游任务
AI.conclusion: PAWS方法能够从大规模第一人称视角视频中有效提取物体关节结构，支持3D关节预测模型微调和机器人操作等下游任务

title: UNIC: Neural Garment Deformation Field for Real-time Clothed Character Animation
summary: Simulating physically realistic garment deformations is an essential task for virtual immersive experience, which is often achieved by physics simulation methods. However, these methods are typically time-consuming, computationally demanding, and require costly hardware, which is not suitable for real-time applications. Recent learning-based methods tried to resolve this problem by training graph neural networks to learn the garment deformation on vertices, which, however, fail to capture the intricate deformation of complex garment meshes with complex topologies. In this paper, we introduce a novel neural deformation field-based method, named UNIC, to animate the garments of an avatar in real time, given the motion sequences. Our key idea is to learn the instance-specific neural deformation field to animate the garment meshes. Such an instance-specific learning scheme does not require UNIC to generalize to new garments but only to new motion sequences, which greatly reduces the difficulty in training and improves the deformation quality. Moreover, neural deformation fields map the 3D points to their deformation offsets, which not only avoids handling topologies of the complex garments but also injects a natural smoothness constraint in the deformation learning. Extensive experiments have been conducted on various kinds of garment meshes to demonstrate the effectiveness and efficiency of UNIC over baseline methods, making it potentially practical and useful in real-world interactive applications like video games.

interest_filter.reason: 该论文提出了一种基于神经变形场的实时服装动画方法，直接涉及物理动画、3D生成和图形学等用户兴趣。论文专注于服装变形模拟，这是物理动画和图形学中的核心问题，并且使用神经场方法进行3D变形，与3D生成技术相关。虽然不直接涉及视频生成或4D重建，但实时角色动画与交互式应用密切相关。

AI.tldr: UNIC提出了一种基于神经变形场的实时服装动画方法，通过实例特定的学习方案避免处理复杂拓扑结构，实现高质量实时服装变形。
AI.motivation: 物理模拟方法计算成本高、硬件要求高，不适合实时应用；现有的基于学习的方法难以捕捉复杂拓扑服装的精细变形。
AI.method: 提出UNIC方法，学习实例特定的神经变形场来动画化服装网格，将3D点映射到变形偏移量，避免处理复杂拓扑并注入自然平滑约束。
AI.result: 在各种服装网格上的广泛实验表明，UNIC在有效性和效率上优于基线方法，适合视频游戏等实时交互应用。
AI.conclusion: UNIC通过神经变形场和实例特定学习方案，实现了高质量实时服装动画，避免了复杂拓扑处理，具有实际应用潜力。

title: TRACE: Object Motion Editing in Videos with First-Frame Trajectory Guidance
summary: We study object motion path editing in videos, where the goal is to alter a target object's trajectory while preserving the original scene content. Unlike prior video editing methods that primarily manipulate appearance or rely on point-track-based trajectory control, which is often challenging for users to provide during inference, especially in videos with camera motion, we offer a practical, easy-to-use approach to controllable object-centric motion editing. We present Trace, a framework that enables users to design the desired trajectory in a single anchor frame and then synthesizes a temporally consistent edited video. Our approach addresses this task with a two-stage pipeline: a cross-view motion transformation module that maps first-frame path design to frame-aligned box trajectories under camera motion, and a motion-conditioned video re-synthesis module that follows these trajectories to regenerate the object while preserving the remaining content of the input video. Experiments on diverse real-world videos show that our method produces more coherent, realistic, and controllable motion edits than recent image-to-video and video-to-video methods.

interest_filter.reason: 该论文专注于视频中的物体运动路径编辑，与用户的"视频生成"和"交互式视频生成"兴趣高度相关。论文提出了一种通过单帧轨迹引导进行物体运动编辑的方法，这直接属于视频生成和编辑的范畴。虽然论文主要关注2D视频编辑而非3D生成，但其核心的物体运动编辑、轨迹控制和视频重合成技术都与用户的视频生成兴趣密切相关。

AI.tldr: Trace框架通过单锚帧轨迹设计实现视频中物体运动路径编辑，包含跨视图运动转换和运动条件视频重合成两阶段流程
AI.motivation: 现有视频编辑方法主要关注外观修改或依赖点跟踪轨迹控制，后者在相机运动视频中难以提供，需要更实用、易用的物体中心运动编辑方法
AI.method: 两阶段框架：1)跨视图运动转换模块将首帧路径设计映射到相机运动下的帧对齐边界框轨迹；2)运动条件视频重合成模块遵循轨迹重新生成物体同时保留视频其余内容
AI.result: 在多样化真实世界视频上的实验表明，相比最近的图像到视频和视频到视频方法，该方法能产生更连贯、真实和可控的运动编辑效果
AI.conclusion: Trace框架提供了一种实用、易用的物体运动路径编辑方法，通过单锚帧轨迹设计实现高质量的视频运动编辑
